KV36|notes_from_pm.value: Standard-bearer, Child of the nursery. Temp. Hatshepsut. Excavated by Loret.
KV36|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.556 (KV36 Mahirper). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`: A=longest with `Excavated by Loret`, B=truncated drops the Loret clause, C=truncated + Ḥatshepsut diacritic variant. Pin agent A's longest verbatim form (matches PM p.556 printed completeness). The Hatshepsut → Ḥatshepsut diacritic correction is applied post-merge by fix_rows.py KV36 entry per the PM postprocessor's underdot policy; ties are resolved at merge time, diacritics at fix_rows time.

KV39|notes_from_pm.value: Uninscribed tomb, attributed to Amenophis I by Weigall in Ann. Serv. xi (1911), pp. 174-5 [12], and id. A Guide to the Antiquities of Upper Egypt, pp. 163-4, but this is not supported by any inscriptional evidence, and does not correspond with the position given in the Abbott Papyrus (cf. Peet, The Great Tomb-Robberies of the Twentieth Egyptian Dynasty, pp. 37-8). See also the tomb of Queen ʿAhmosi Nefertere, infra, p. 599.
KV39|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.559 (KV39). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`. Even agent A's longest form was a TRUNCATION — PM p.559 prints the full paragraph through `... infra, p. 599.` (egyptologist verified printed source PR #151 review). Pinned value extended to PM-verbatim form: includes the load-bearing Abbott Papyrus + Peet citation chain (independent reason to doubt the Amenophis I attribution) AND the `infra, p. 599` cross-reference linking KV39 to DAN-AhmosiNefertere. The `Ḥ` underdot on `ʿAhmosi`/`ʿAḥmosi` is layered post-merge by fix_rows.py per the underdot policy.

KV42|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.559 (KV42 Tuthmosis II). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`Attribution uncertain (?).` (with terminal period), B=null (agent dropped the uncertainty marker), C=`Attribution uncertain (?)` (no terminal period). Pin agent A's value with terminal period — fix_rows.py KV42 entry then rewrites to `(?). Excavated by Loret` per PM p.559 prints `42. TUTHMOSIS II (?)` headword convention; the override keeps merge output stable, fix_rows handles the canonical rewrite.

QV47|notes_from_pm.value: daughter of Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa and Sit-ḏḥout. Dyn. XVII.
QV47|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.755 (QV47 ʿAhmosi). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`: A=Sit-ḍḥout no Bibl (WRONG-CONSONANT — `ḍ` d-underdot, used in Semitic transliteration), B=Sit-ḍḥout with `(Bibl. i, 1st ed. p. 49.)` (same wrong consonant), C=Sit-ḏḥout with Bibl (CORRECT consonant `ḏ` d-bar, the standard Egyptological transliteration of the d-emphatic, same character used for the deity Ḏḥuti/Thoth). Egyptologist printed-source review on PR #151 verified PM p.755 prints `Sit-ḏḥout` with `ḏ` (d-bar), NOT `ḍ` (d-underdot). The value pinned here uses the egyptologically-correct consonant — agents A and B were wrong, agent C was right. The bare-form-without-Bibl is intentional: fix_rows.py QV47 entry restores the Bibl. ribbon post-merge per the egyptologist-reviewer's printed-source restoration. The `Ē` macron on `Seḳenenrēʿ` is layered by fix_rows.py per the diacritic policy.

QV74|notes_from_pm.value: Great King's mother and King's wife. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 2, HAY, No. 7.)
QV74|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.767 (QV74 Tentopet). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm` because they all attempted to synthesize main-text + footnote 1 content into a single notes blob. Egyptologist printed-source review on PR #151 surfaced this as a rule-1 (provenance) violation: PM p.767 main text reads `74. QUEEN TENTŌPET [cartouche], Great King's mother and King's wife.¹ (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 2, HAY, No. 7.)` and footnote 1 carries the Wife/mother/daughter-of-Ramesses-IV genealogy with three independent citations (Gauthier / Černý / Seele). Pinning a synthesized form put non-PM-verbatim prose into `notes_from_pm`. Fixed: pin PM main-text VERBATIM only. The footnote 1 genealogy + per-citation chain is dropped from `notes_from_pm` here; the schema currently has no `notes_footnote` / `notes_genealogy` field, so the genealogy is tracked for restoration via follow-up issue (schema split). The `Ō` macron on `TENTŌPET` headword convention is recorded in `occupant_name`, out of scope for this notes override.

SWV-HatshepsutSouth|notes_from_pm.value: See also Tomb 20, supra, p. 546. Sarcophagus as Queen-Consort.
SWV-HatshepsutSouth|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.591 (SWV-HatshepsutSouth). Agents split 1/1/1: A=bare cross-ref + bare Sarcophagus mention, B=adds `South Tomb of Ḥatshepsut.` headline + `quartzite` material, C=A's structure + `quartzite` material. Pin agent A's bare form — the `South Tomb of Ḥatshepsut.` headline is generated downstream from `valley` + `tomb_id`, and the `quartzite` material detail is layered by fix_rows.py SWV-HatshepsutSouth entry (PM p.591 prints quartzite + Cairo Mus. Ent. number that fix_rows expands).

DAN-AhmosiHenutempet|notes_from_pm.value: Daughter of ʿAhhotp (wife of King Seqenenreʿ-Taʿa). Coffin possibly from here.
DAN-AhmosiHenutempet|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.604 (DAN-AhmosiHenutempet). Agents split 1/1/1: A=ʿAhhotp + Seqenenreʿ + Coffin clause, B=ʿAhhotp + Seqenenreʿ no Coffin clause, C=ʿAḥḥotp + Seḳenenreʿ diacritic-rich variant + Coffin clause. Pin agent A's longest form with the load-bearing `Coffin possibly from here` provenance hedge; the underdot diacritics on ʿAḥḥotp / Seḳenenreʿ are post-merge corrections layered by fix_rows.py per the PM postprocessor's underdot policy.

DAN-AhmosiNefertere|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.599 (DAN-AhmosiNefertere). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`: A=bare attribution headline only, B=bare Carter/Winlock/Černý attribution chain (NO headline), C=full headline + abbreviated Carter chain. Pin agent A's bare headline — the Carter / Winlock / Černý attribution chain is extended post-merge by fix_rows.py DAN-AhmosiNefertere entry per PM p.599 printed prose.

DAN-AntefSekhemreHeruhirmaet|occupant_na.rationale: PM I.2 p.603 (DAN-AntefSekhemreHeruhirmaet). Agents split 1/1/1 on `occupant_name`: A=`Antef (Sekhemreʿ-Heruhirmaʿet)`, B=`Antef (Sekhemreʿ-Heruḥirmaʿet)` (with ḥ underdot variant), C=`Antef` (bare, no parenthetical — agent dropped the disambiguator). Pin agent A's verbatim form with the parenthetical preserved; the Heruhirmaʿet → Heruḥirmaʿet diacritic correction is layered by fix_rows.py per the PM underdot policy. Bare `Antef` is wrong — without the parenthetical Sekhemreʿ-Heruhirmaʿet, this row collides with multiple other Antefs in PM's Dyn XVII clusters.

DAN-Aqhor|occupant_name.rationale: PM I.2 p.605 (DAN-Aqhor; PM headword printed as `ʿAḲ-ḤOR`). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`ʿAq-hor` (no underdot, lowercase 'h'), B=`ʿAq-Hor` (no underdot, capital 'H'), C=`ʿAḳ-hor` (underdot ḳ). Pin agent A's `ʿAq-hor` — the underdot ḳ correction (matching PM headword `ʿAḲ-ḤOR`) is layered by fix_rows.py DAN-Aqhor entry, which also handles the tomb_id rename from the historical `DAN-Ahhor` form to the canonical `DAN-Aqhor`. Override matches pre-fix-rows merge output that fix_rows expects to mutate.

TT4|notes_from_pm.value: Chiseller of Amun in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 106.) Parents, Thonufer, Chiseller of Amun in the Khenu, and Maʿetnefert. Wives, Nefertere and Ḥenutmeḥyt.
TT4|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.11 (TT4 Ḳen). Agents split 1/1/1 on text-layer-noise variants for the parent name (`Thoniifer` vs `Thonufer`) and on whether to add a redundant trailing period after the bibliographic close-paren. Pin a stable post-merge form so the merge step has a deterministic answer for the tie; egyptologist printed-source corrections (macron restoration on `Amūn` ×2 + `Thonūfer`, plus the medial-underdot drop on `Ḥenutmeḥyt` → `Ḥenutmehyt`) are layered post-merge by `CHUNK9_CORRECTIONS` in `fix_rows.py`. The pinned-merge value here corresponds to the chunk-9 PR's first-pass merge output before the egyptologist's printed-source review; the final on-disk value comes from CHUNK9_CORRECTIONS, which the per-row test asserts. Keeping merge-time and fix_rows-time overrides separate maintains the audit-trail boundary (merge resolves agent ties; fix_rows applies reviewer-cited PM-faithful corrections).

DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjhuti|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.604 (DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjhuti). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`Djhuti`, B=`Djehuty` (vowel-extended Anglicised form), C=`Djḥuti` (underdot ḥ variant). Pin agent A's `Djhuti` matching the canonical PM tomb_id form (CHUNK7_RENAMES: `DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjehuti` → `DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjhuti`). Egyptologist printed-source review on PR #151 verified PM p.604 actually prints sentence-form `QUEEN MENTUḤOTP¹, wife of King Ḏḥuti` (the `¹` is footnote anchor, NOT numeral I) — the consonant in `Ḏḥuti` is `Ḏ` (d with bar/macron, the standard Egyptological transliteration of the d-emphatic, same character as in `Ḏḥwty`/Thoth), NOT `Ḍ` (d with underdot, used in some Semitic transliteration systems). They are different consonants representing different sounds. Wrong-consonant risk: a row keyed on `Ḍḥuti` would never match against TLA / Trismegistos / museum data using `Ḏḥuti`. The underdot/d-bar correction `Djhuti` → `Ḏḥuti` is layered by fix_rows.py per the PM postprocessor's underdot policy.
